tasks.name: identity
tasks.top_friction: README documents `clickup whoami` but the command does not exist, forcing discovery through `clickup status`.

tasks.name: list teams
tasks.top_friction: --format json flag is silently ignored; CLI always renders a Rich table on workspace list.

tasks.name: hierarchy tree
tasks.top_friction: --format json silently ignored on discover hierarchy; agents get tree art instead of structured data.

tasks.name: my tasks
tasks.top_friction: README documents `clickup whoami` that does not exist; task mine returns empty without explanation.

tasks.name: sort updated
tasks.top_friction: Hierarchy drill-down requires 3 chained discover calls to reach a list ID.

tasks.name: create+delete
tasks.top_friction: No default list ID + 3-hop discovery required before first task create.

tasks.name: modify-if-passed
tasks.top_friction: --format is a top-level flag not a subcommand flag, causing a non-obvious error on first attempt.

tasks.name: search
tasks.top_friction: workspace-id is required but not auto-detected when only one workspace exists, forcing an extra lookup command.

tasks.name: task get + comments
tasks.top_friction: 4-command hierarchy walk needed before you can address any task.

tasks.name: bulk export
tasks.top_friction: Three-hop ID discovery requires chaining discover ids three times before any real work can begin.

tasks.name: most-active
tasks.top_friction: --format is a global-only flag; placing it after the subcommand silently fails with no helpful error.

tasks.name: onboarding
tasks.top_friction: setup wizard interactive prompt blocks non-interactive AI agents -- needs --yes flag.

verdict_changes: Task 9: PARTIAL -> PASS (comments + expanded fields fully accessible)
Task 4: PASS -> PARTIAL (now flags 'why is mine empty' as friction)
newly_resolved: spinner pollution (zero mentions in eval2 — was top friction in 7 of 12 last run)
.env auto-load failures (zero mentions — user-global ~/.config/clickup-toolkit/.env now found regardless of cwd)
newly_surfaced: README still claims top-level `whoami` (it lives at `config whoami`)
`setup run` interactive prompt blocks agents (no --yes flag)
`task search` doesn't auto-detect singleton workspace (inconsistent with `task mine`)

next_moves: .env-source labeling in `clickup status` (so agents can self-diagnose token issues)
Migrate remaining commands to `clickup/cli/output.py` renderers (workspace, list, discover hierarchy)
